goal: Add a tree view mode for workorders on the existing board page while preserving board styles, right drawer workflows and server-side access control.
global_constraints: Do not build a separate workorder data access path for the tree.
Tree mode must use the same visible_workorders_queryset foundation as board mode.
Customer branch visibility must be enforced server-side, not only hidden in templates.
Creation and editing must continue to use the right drawer partials.
Preserve current board visual language: toolbar, work-card, badge, chip and drawer styles.
Do not introduce an Organization model in this slice.
Temporary scripts, logs and screenshots belong in .local/.
Update .desc.json and PROJECT_STRUCTURE.yaml after structural changes.
ux_principles: Use a segmented view switcher instead of separate navigation.
Use a treegrid-like layout because each row needs hierarchy plus metadata.
Keep detail work in the right drawer; do not nest full details under each row.
Auto-expand ancestors when search returns matching workorders.
Use visible actions for create/open; do not rely only on hover actions.
Make keyboard focus and selected workorder visually distinct.
cross_cutting_acceptance: Tree mode shows organization, departments, devices and workorders.
Workorders without device are shown under a stable fallback node.
Customer cannot see, open, edit or create outside their department branch.
Filters and search work in both modes.
Right drawer detail/create/edit flows work from tree mode.
Accessibility smoke test covers expand/collapse and row opening from keyboard.
verification_commands: E2E_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8000 E2E_USERNAME=chief_manager E2E_PASSWORD=... npm run test:e2e -- --project=chromium --grep "workorder tree"
implementation_results: Added department_branch access scope for workorders.
Changed customer default and runtime role rules to department_branch.
Restricted workorder department and device form fields to the user's visible branch.
Added server-side workorder tree projection in apps/workorders/tree.py.
Added board/tree view switching on the existing workorders page.
Preserved right drawer detail/create/edit flows from tree mode.
Added tree CSS and keyboard expand/collapse JavaScript.
Added unit/view tests and a skipped-when-unconfigured Playwright e2e spec.
deferred_decisions: Whether to add a first-class Organization model.
Whether to add Department.kind for explicit labels such as organization, subdivision and ward.
Whether tree nodes should support drag-and-drop or batch operations.
Whether very large trees need server-side lazy loading or client-side virtualization.
